en: Now three days after Festus had arrived in the province, he went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.
zh: 非斯都到任三天之後，就從該撒利亞去耶路撒冷。

en: And the chief priests and the principal men of the Jews laid out their case against Paul, and they urged him,
zh: 祭司長和猶太人的首領，到他面前控告保羅。他們又要求非斯都，

en: asking as a favor against Paul that he summon him to Jerusalem- because they were planning an ambush to kill him on the way.
zh: 求他恩准對付保羅，把保羅解來耶路撒冷，他們好埋伏在路上殺死他。

en: Festus replied that Paul was being kept at Caesarea and that he himself intended to go there shortly.
zh: 非斯都回答：“保羅現今押在該撒利亞，我就要回到那裡去。”

en: "So," said he, "let the men of authority among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong about the man, let them bring charges against him."
zh: 又說：“你們中間有權勢的，和我一同去吧！那人若有甚麼不是，他們就可以告他。”

en: After he stayed among them not more than eight or ten days, he went down to Caesarea. And the next day he took his seat on the tribunal and ordered Paul to be brought.
zh: 非斯都在他們那裡大約逗留了不過十天八天，就回到該撒利亞去。第二天就開庭，吩咐把保羅帶來。

en: When he had arrived, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him, bringing many and serious charges against him that they could not prove.
zh: 保羅一到，那些從耶路撒冷來的猶太人，就站在他周圍，提出許多嚴重的控告，可是所控告的他們都不能證實。

en: Paul argued in his defense, "Neither against the law of the Jews, nor against the temple, nor against Caesar have I committed any offense."
zh: 保羅申辯說：“無論對猶太人的律法、聖殿或凱撒，我都沒有罪。”

en: But Festus, wishing to do the Jews a favor, said to Paul, "Do you wish to go up to Jerusalem and there be tried on these charges before me?"
zh: 但非斯都為要討好猶太人，就問保羅：“你願意去耶路撒冷，讓我在那裡審問這事嗎？”

en: But Paul said, "I am standing before Caesar's tribunal, where I ought to be tried. To the Jews I have done no wrong, as you yourselves know very well.
zh: 保羅說：“我現在站在凱撒的審判臺前，這裡是我應當受審的地方。我對猶太人並沒有作過甚麼不對的事，這是你清楚知道的。

en: If then I am a wrongdoer and have committed anything for which I deserve to die, I do not seek to escape death. But if there is nothing to their charges against me, no one can give me up to them. I appeal to Caesar."
zh: 我若作過不對的事，犯過甚麼該死的罪，就是死我也不推辭。不過，如果這些人告我的事不是真的，誰也不可以把我送給他們。我要向凱撒上訴。”

en: Then Festus, when he had conferred with his council, answered, "To Caesar you have appealed; to Caesar you shall go."
zh: 非斯都同議會商量後，答覆說：“你既然要向凱撒上訴，可以到凱撒那裡去。”

en: Now when some days had passed, Agrippa the king and Bernice arrived at Caesarea and greeted Festus.
zh: 過了一些日子，亞基帕王和百尼基到該撒利亞來，問候非斯都。

en: And as they stayed there many days, Festus laid Paul's case before the king, saying, "There is a man left prisoner by Felix,
zh: 他們在那裡逗留了許多天，非斯都把保羅的案情向王陳明，說：“這裡有一個囚犯，是腓力斯留下來的。

en: and when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews laid out their case against him, asking for a sentence of condemnation against him.
zh: 我在耶路撒冷的時候，祭司長和猶太人的長老控告他，要求把他定罪。

en: I answered them that it was not the custom of the Romans to give up anyone before the accused met the accusers face to face and had opportunity to make his defense concerning the charge laid against him.
zh: 我答覆他們說，被告還沒有和原告當面對證，又沒有機會為所控的罪申辯，就被送給對方，羅馬人沒有這個規例。

en: So when they came together here, I made no delay, but on the next day took my seat on the tribunal and ordered the man to be brought.
zh: 後來他們都到了這裡，我沒有耽延時間，第二天就開庭，吩咐把那人提出來。

en: When the accusers stood up, they brought no charge in his case of such evils as I supposed.
zh: 原告都站在那裡，他們所控訴他的，並不是我所意料的惡事。

en: Rather they had certain points of dispute with him about their own religion and about a certain Jesus, who was dead, but whom Paul asserted to be alive.
zh: 他們與他爭辯的，只是他們的宗教問題；還論到一位已經死了的耶穌，保羅卻說他是活著的。

en: Being at a loss how to investigate these questions, I asked whether he wanted to go to Jerusalem and be tried there regarding them.
zh: 關於這些爭論，我不知道怎樣處理，就問他願不願意上耶路撒冷去為這些事在那裡受審。

en: But when Paul had appealed to be kept in custody for the decision of the emperor, I ordered him to be held until I could send him to Caesar."
zh: 但保羅要求把他留下，等待皇上裁判，我就下令把他押起來，等候解往凱撒那裡。”

en: Then Agrippa said to Festus, "I would like to hear the man myself." "Tomorrow," said he, "you will hear him."
zh: 亞基帕對非斯都說：“我也想聽聽這人說些甚麼。”非斯都說：“明天就請你來聽。”

en: So on the next day Agrippa and Bernice came with great pomp, and they entered the audience hall with the military tribunes and the prominent men of the city. Then, at the command of Festus, Paul was brought in.
zh: 第二天，亞基帕和百尼基威風凜凜地來了，同千夫長和城中的顯要進了廳堂。非斯都吩咐一聲，就有人把保羅提出來。

en: And Festus said, "King Agrippa and all who are present with us, you see this man about whom the whole Jewish people petitioned me, both in Jerusalem and here, shouting that he ought not to live any longer.
zh: 非斯都說：“亞基帕王和在座的各位，請看這個人。耶路撒冷和本地的猶太人都向我請求，嚷著說這個人不該活著。

en: But I found that he had done nothing deserving death. And as he himself appealed to the emperor, I decided to go ahead and send him.
zh: 我查明他沒有犯甚麼該死的罪，但是他自己既然要向皇上上訴，我就決定把他解去。

en: But I have nothing definite to write to my lord about him. Therefore I have brought him before you all, and especially before you, King Agrippa, so that, after we have examined him, I may have something to write.
zh: 關於這個人，我沒有甚麼確實的事可以呈奏主上的。所以把他帶到你們面前，特別是你亞基帕王面前，為要在審查之後，有所陳奏。

en: For it seems to me unreasonable, in sending a prisoner, not to indicate the charges against him."
zh: 因為我認為解送囚犯，不指明他的罪狀，是不合理的。”
